steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Rhetorical Synthesis question asking for a generalization about the Hina Hanta archive. A generalization draws a broad conclusion from multiple specific data points; the correct choice must combine information from at least two notes to make a claim about the archive as a whole, not merely report a single fact.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "The Choctaw name for the clay cup is *isht ishko*.": Reports one isolated detail from a single note. It says nothing about the archive overall. ✗
  • "Included in the Hina Hanta online archive is a clay cup.": States a single fact about one item in the archive. It does not generalize about the archive’s scope or character. ✗
  • "The horsehair stickball collar (*innuchi*) was made in 2016.": Reports one isolated fact from a single note. It says nothing about the archive overall. ✗
  • "Not all artifacts in the Hina Hanta archive are from the 1800s.": Draws a broad conclusion by combining the 1891 cup and the 2016 stickball collar, showing the archive spans beyond the 1800s. This is a genuine generalization about the archive. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
